description: Fix stale docs/wiki/ and workspace/heartbeats/ path references in harness-audit and skill-lint skills so audit sub-agents inspect real directories; add a guard probe so the drift cannot silently return.

userStories.title: Correct stale paths in harness-audit/SKILL.md
userStories.description: As an orchestrator running /harness-audit, I want the audit sub-agents to inspect the real wiki/ and crons/ directories so the audit reports accurate findings instead of false negatives.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace all 4 `workspace/heartbeats/` occurrences (lines ~59, 179, 207, 306) with `crons/`, rewording adjacent prose so 'heartbeat files' reads as 'cron definitions in `crons/`' where the phrasing would otherwise be wrong (incl. the Key paths table row label: 'Heartbeats | crons/')
Do NOT touch the apps/docs/, workspace/.claude/skills/, or bare MEMORY.md lines (out of scope per Non-Goals)
The audit-area intent is preserved (wiki-utilization and heartbeat/cron-health checks still make sense against the new paths)

userStories.title: Correct stale path in skill-lint/SKILL.md
userStories.description: As an orchestrator running /skill-lint, I want the heartbeat-coverage bonus signal to look at the real crons/ directory so the lint scoring is not permanently short-circuited.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace the single `workspace/heartbeats/` occurrence (line ~216) with `crons/` in .claude/skills/skill-lint/SKILL.md, rewording the bullet so it reads 'no heartbeat/cron reference in `crons/`'
Leave skill-lint lines 26 and 225 (`workspace/.claude/skills/`) untouched — that is intentional dual-scope, not a restructure bug

userStories.title: Add guard probe evals/probes/skill-paths.sh
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a deterministic probe that REGRESSES if the two retired renamed-directory tokens reappear in the skills tree, so this drift cannot silently return.
acceptanceCriteria: Create evals/probes/skill-paths.sh, executable (chmod +x), with shebang + `set -euo pipefail` and repo-root detection via ${BASH_SOURCE[0]} (no hardcoded /home/sandbox/harness)

Match command exactly: `grep -rnE 'docs/wiki/|workspace/heartbeats/' "$ROOT/.claude/skills/" | grep -v 'harness-context/SKILL.md'` — non-empty result exits 1 (REGRESSION) and prints the offending lines
Mirror the 3-state oracle of evals/probes/eval-gate.sh: PASS=0, REGRESSION=1, SKIPPED=2; if .claude/skills/ is absent, exit 2 and print SKIPPED
On the clean post-fix tree, `bash evals/probes/skill-paths.sh` exits 0 and prints PASS
Temporarily reintroduce a retired token into a skill file, confirm the probe exits 1 and prints REGRESSION, then revert and confirm it exits 0 again
`bash -n evals/probes/skill-paths.sh` passes (and shellcheck is clean if available)

userStories.title: Record the fix in CHANGELOG.md
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want the change recorded in the changelog so the fix is discoverable in release notes.
acceptanceCriteria: Append a one-line bullet to the EXISTING `### Fixed` block under `## [Unreleased]` in CHANGELOG.md (do NOT create a second `### Fixed` heading)
Bullet is imperative mood and links issue #43 as [#43](https://github.com/ryaneggz/openharness/issues/43)
`grep -c '^### Fixed' CHANGELOG.md` count is unchanged (no duplicate heading introduced under Unreleased)
